cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. So the question is multiple choice with options A to D. The big project was a book report, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something a little different. The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. After the recitation, the class applauded, and the teacher praised her for choosing a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. This made the author feel confident for the first time. The teacher's comment focuses on the choice of passage and delivery, not memory. So probably not A. B. To make her earn respect. The author was shy and introverted, maybe teased. Reciting a passage could help understand the text, but the teacher's comment was about the choice and delivery. The author had already chosen a passage she considered best. The focus was more on presentation and confidence. Not sure if it's about understanding. D. To build relationships. The author was isolated, reading alone. By having her recite, maybe to help her interact with others. But the teacher's comment was about the performance, not about relationships.
